Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-00-033
Dear Ms. Gillan:
ThisletterisinresponsetoyourrequestforadviceonbehalfoftheCaliforniaPublic
Empioyees, Retirement System c.Carnns,) regarding the poritical Reform Act (the "Act")r as
amenaeA by Senate Bill 1753 (Stats' 1998, ch' 923)'
QUESTIONS
1. Do the campaign requirements in Chapter 4, Article 1 (Sections 84100-84105) apply
to candidates for the CaIPERS board?
2. If so, do the contributions received before the effective date of SB 1753 trigger a duty
to comply with Sections g4100 and g4101? Do the contributions received before the adoption of
Regulations 18451 *J rg+sz trigqer a duty to comply with Sections 84100 and 84101?
3. When must a current elected board membef file a statement of intention to be a
candidate (Form 501)?
4. When must a current elected board member open a campaign bank account?
r Govenrment Code sections g1000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18996, of the California Code of Regulations'
2 As used here, *elected board member" refers to the six members of the CaIPERS board who are elected
to the board. It does rrot refe, to ttre State Treasurer, the state contoller, or any other member of the board who
holds elective oflice separate from the board since SB 1753 did not change their duties under the AcL


File No. A-00-033
Page2
5. Do the campaign restrictions in Chapter 4, Article 3 (Sections 84300-84307, excluding
Section 84308) apply to candidates for the calPERS board?
6. Does the inter-candidate transfer ban in Section 85304 apply to candidates for the
CaIPERS board?
7. Arethe contributions received in a CaIPERS election subject to the contribution
restrictions in Section 84308?
g. Does the special election contibution limit in Section85305 appiy to candidates in a
calpERS election held outside the nonnal election cycle?
9. Are the elected board members of CaIPERS subject to the revolving door laws in
Section 87406(c)?
10. Are the elected board members of CaIPERS subject to the prohibition on mass
mailing at public expense in Section 89001?
ll.Ifso,mayCa1PERSdistributeagroupphotothatincludeseveryCa1PERSboard
member under the roster listing exception of Regulation 18901(b)(1) or (bx8)?
12.WhendoestheActrequireanelectedboardmemberofCalPERStofilehisorher
arurual statement of economic interest (Form 700)?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Yes. As "candidates" defined in Section 82007, CaIPERS candidates must comply
with the campaign requirements in chapter 4, Article 1 (sections 84100-84105)'
2. Contributions made or received before January 1, 1999, to influence a CaIPERS
election do not count toward qualifying a calPERS candidate as a committee' However' if a
CalpERS'candidate received contributtns of $1,000 or more after that date' the candidate must
have a freasurer, and file a statement ;f "rt*rir"1ion 
within 10 days of qualifying as a committee'
3. A curent elected board member must file a Form 501 if the member intends to run in
the next election. The member must file this form before soliciting or accepting any
contributions, or *^*i"; * "*p*ai*.s 
from personal funds on beharf of his or her candidacy'
4. A curent elected board member must open a campaign bank account after he or she
files a Form 501 and before making any expendiflres'


File No. A-00-033
Page 3
5. yes. As..candidates" defined in Section 82007, CaIPERS candidates must comply
with Sections 84300-8 $47 .
6. The inter-candidate transfer ban does not apply to CaIPERS candidates, unless the
CalpERS board has imposed valid contribution limits applicable to CaIPERS elections'
7. No. Contributions made or received to influence a CaIPERS election are not subject
to the contribution restrictions in Section 84308 since they are not "contributions" as defined by
Section Sa308(aX6).
g. No. The special election contribution limits do not apply to CaIPERS elections held
outside the nonnal eiection cycle'
9. yes. As..elected state officers," the six elected members of the CaIPERS board are
subject to Section 87a06(c).
10. yes. As..elected officers," the mass mailing prohibition in Section 89001 applies to
the six elected members of the CaIPERS board'
ll.No.Therosterlistingexceptiondoesnotapplytophotographs.
12. An elected board member must file his or her Form 700 by April 1 each year'
FACTS
calpERS administers the public retirement system for califomia's state employees'
classified school employees, and imployees of public agencies that elect to contract with
CalpERS. fn this capacity, baPERS Uofas in trust and is responsible for the investnent of
approximately $1a0iinion. The calpERs, Board of Administration invests the bulk of these
frrnds in the puuri. -*t.ir; it also invests some of these funds in private equity and real estate'
Bystatute,theCa1PERSboardconsistsofthirteenmembers'Ofthese'sixareelected
from the memb"rship ,nder the board's supervision. (S_ection 20100(g).) of the remaining
seven board members, two, the State Treasr:rer and the State controrler, hord ofEce by virtue of
holding statewide elected office. (Section 20100(c) and (d)') The rest of the board members
hold office by appointnent' (Section 20100(a)' (b)' (e)' and (f)')
The six elected calPERS board members are elected by the following constituencies
(1) two are elected by members of the system (i.e., elected by all active and retired calPERS
members, or .'members at large"); (Zj ont is elected by-active state members; (3) one is elected
by active school members; (ai one is elected by active local (except school) members; and
1i; one is elected by retired members' (Section 20100(g)')


File No. A-00-033
Page4
Board members are elected for four-year terms. These terms are staggered. In the first
year of the cycle, calPERS holds an election for the three seats representing active state' school'
and locar members. In the second year, carpERS hords an election for the retiree representative'
There is no election in the third year, and, in the fourth year, calPERS holds an election for the
two member-at-large positions. If a seat becomes vacant during a term' CaIPERS conducts a
special election. gefore sB 1753, CaIPERS developed all of its own election procedures and
under the Act, cand.idates are subject to several rules' For example' they must file a
statement of intention to be a candidate, and maintain a separate campaign bank acco*nt for each
office to which they seek election. (Sections 85200, 85201.) In addition, they must file various
campaign-related reports concerning the monetary activities of their committees' (See e'g''
Sections 84100-8410 5,84225.) Finaily, they must comply with laws that restrict the handling
and receipt of contributions, the making of expenditures, and the distribution of carrrpaign
literatr.re. (Sections 84300-84309')
Before lggg,candidates in CaIPERS elections were not subject to the Act,s provisions
regulating election ,*tpAgD, . (See KoppesAdvice Letter' No' A-g4-l2l')3 However' in 1998'
the Legisrature passed sg izs: tn"t, u*ong other thinqs, amended the Act to require candidates
for the CaIPERS board to file srmFai$ staltements with the Secretary of State' (Stats' 1998' ch'
923.) Therefore, like other candidates for elective office, candidates for the CaIPERS board are
now required to report the monetary activities of their campaign committees' (Section 84225')
calpERS candidates, however, are not subject to the same'disclosure rules as other candidates'
Sections4225,whichimposesthenewreportingrequirementson.CalPERScandidates,
exempts these candidates from chapter +, Article z of the Act (Sections 84200-84',.16',-)- -^,-
(Section 84225@)t Along with gther parts of the Acq Chapter 4' Article 2 sets forth campatgn
discrosrire requirements appricable to arl candidates. The disclosure obligations that apply only
to calpERS candidates are set forth in Regulations 1g451 and 1g452. Regurations 18451 and
lggs1impose limited reporting requiremJnts relative to other candidates'a
policies.
A}{ALYSIS
Campaign Reporting Provisions and Related Restrictions

in law has rendered its advice inapplicable'
,
. Under these regulations, CalpERS candidates must file two semi-annual statements disclosing only the
information specified in the regulatiou'

Page 5
In addition to adding Section 84225 to the Act, SB 1753 amended the Act's definitions of
..elective offrce,, and "elective state office" to include elected CaIPERS members. (Sections
1ZOZ3,gZO24.) your questions concern the consequences of including elected CaIPERS
members in the Act's aennitions of "elective office" and "elective state office." The legislative
. history of SB 1753 does not reveal the legislative intent behind amending these definitions's In
addition, at a public meeting in October t999, tt " 
Commission declined to narrowly interpret
SB i753 by rejecting propo-sed Regulation 18450.6 The proposed regulation would have
=-pro.ria"d that the eliied members of CaIPERS hold an "electivedEce" and an "elective state
offi.." solely forpurposes of Section 84225' 
" 
2
Therefore, under a plain reading of the two statutes, the six elected members of the
CalpERS board are subjecito all of the Act's statutes using the terms "elective office" or
"elective state office," Jxcept Chapter 4, Article 2 as described in Section8a225.ft)'
The first consequence of the new law concerns the Act's definition of "candidate'"
Section gzoo1 defines a..candidate" as "an individual who is listed on the ballot for election to
any elective office." (Emphasis added.) Thus, we conclude that anyone listed as a candidate on a
caIpERS ballot is a.,candidate,, for purposes of the Act, except as limited by Section 84225@)
andRegulations 18451 and 18452'
Section gzool also defines "candidate" to include anyone who receives a contribution or
makes an expenditure '\rith a view to bringing about his or her nomination or election to any
erective ofice.,, @mphasis added.) undeittris second definition, an individual who intends to
become a candidate will be considered one if he or she receives a contribution or makes an
expendinre. This is true even if the individual has not announced his or her candidacy' (Id')
Accordingly, anyone who intends to run for the calPERs board will be considered a "candidate"
when he or she receives a contribution or makes a campaign expendihrre'7
ln general, a "contribution" is a payment made to a"candidate" for the purpose of
influencing the election of the candidate, including a candidate's use of personal funds' (Section
82015; Regulation 18215.) In addition, " puy*.J male by a candidate to influence the election
of the candidate is an "expenditure." (section 8})25;Regulation 18225') Since we have
5 See Senate Rules Cornmittee, Unfinished Business, Analysis of SB 1753 (August 13, 1998) (stating that
sB 1753 ..includes members elected to tue go.reJing uoura oi_c"rp-gRs in the definition of 'erective ofhce' and
.elective state offrce' forl rrpor", of the Political RJforrr Ac!" but providinB no other information)'
6 The Fppc,s expertise of the Act is entitled to great weight unless clearly erroneous or unauthorized'
(People v. Snyder (2000) 92 Cal'Rptr'2d'734,737 ')

to catPERS Regulation 554.3 for an election couducted under Section 20096, which regulates calPERS elections'
BytheexpresstermsofRegulationlS45l,thisdefinitiononlyappliestoSection34225aadRegulatioulS4S2'

concluded that candidates for the CaIPERS board are "candidates" as defined in Section 82007,
the payments they receive and spend for the election are "contributions" and "expendifures" for
porpor.t of the Act, except as limited by Section 842250)'
Question 1 - ChaPter 4, Article 1
you have asked whether candidates for the CaIPERS board are subject to the campaign
requirements in Chapter 4, Article 1 (Sections 84100-84105) of the Act'
Section 84100 requires every "committee" to have a teasur"r, who must veriff and sign
the committee's campaign statements. In addition, Sections 84101-84103 require recipient
committees to file a statement of organization (Form 410) with the Secretary of State within 10
days after it has qualified as a committee. A candidate who receives contibutions of $1'000 or
more in a calendar year qualifies as a recipient committee. (Section 82013(a).) As "candidates"
defined by Section'gzool,candidates for the CaIPERS board are subject to Sections 84100-
g4103 if ihey receive contributions of $1,000 or more in a calendar year'
Section g4104 requires candidates to maintain records that are necessary to comply with
the Act. In addition, Section 84105 requires candidates and cornmittees who have received
$5,000 or more from a contributor to notiry the contributor that he or she may be required to file
as a major donor commiffee. (See Section82013(c).) Sections 84104 and 84105 apply to all
.,candidates.,, Since candidates for the CaIPERS board are "candidates" for purposes of the Act
they are subject to these provisions'
Question 2 - Sections 84100 and 84101
Section g4100 requires a CaIPERS candidate to have a treasurer once the candidate
qualifles as a committee. Under Section 84100, a candidate may serve as a treasurer' In
addition, Section g4101 requires the candidate to file a statement of organization within 10 days
after the candidate qualifies as a committee. A candidate qualifies as a cornmittee if he or she
;;;;r """*u*i"* 
of $1,000 or'more in a calendar year' (Section 82013(a)')
you would like to know whether a contribution made to inlluence a CaIPERS election
niggers a calPERS candidate's duty to comply with Sections 84100 and 84101 if it was received
before January 1, 1999, the effective date ofsb n53.E Since the Act did not regulate these
payments before that date, they do not trigger a duty to comply with Sections 84100 and 84101'
s you have also asked about contributions made after the new law took effect, but before the Commission
considercd adopting a narrowing regulation. Any activity in relation to these payments is past couduct' The
Commission does not provide advice ."grraiog-pu,i"o"i""t Cn"gulation 18'29') Moreover' the Commission is
not authorized to waive a statute. (Caminetti u.'S,o'" Uut' tifi ti' Co' Q9a\ 52 Cal'App'2 d32l'325') Therefore'
whether a CalpERS candidate should t ur" .o11rftiJ with th; ect durini that time is not a matter we can address'


File No. A-00-033
PageT
However, any contributions received by a CaIPERS candidate after January l, 1999, do
count toward qualifying the candidate as a committee. Therefore, current board members who
intend to rrm in the nex-t election must file a statement of organization within 10 days after
receiving qualifying contributions totaling $1,000 or more.
Question 3 - Section 85200
Section g5200 requires an individual, who intends to be a "candidate for elective office,"
to file a Statement of Intention to be a candidate (Form 501) with the Secretary of State. As
prospective "candidates for elective office," current mernbers of CaIPERS who intend to run in
the next election are subject to this requirement. (See Section 82007') They must file the form
after they decide to nrn ibr reelection, but before they solicit or receive any conffibutions, or
make erpenditures from personal funds on behalf of their candidacy'
Question 4 - Section 85201
Section g5201 requires an individual, who intends to be a "candidate for eiective office"'
to establish a campaign bank account. As prospective "candidates for elective office," cu:rent
members of CaIpERS who intend to run inthe next election are subject to this requirement' (See
Section g2007.) To comply, the candidate must estabrish a campaign bank accorurt after filing a
statement of intention, buttefore making an expenditure. In addition, all monetary contributions
or roans made to the candidate must be Jeposit.a in the account.e (Section 85201(c).) Any
personal funds that the candidate intends to ,r. for his or her election must also be deposited in
tt, campaigu bank account prior to expendittue. (Section 85201(d)')
Section g5201, however, does not require aprospective candidate to open a campaign-
bank account if he or she only receives nonmonetary contributions. In addition, Section 85201
does not apply to candidates who will not receive contributions from others, and will make
expenditui". of p"rronal funds of less than $1,000. (Section 85201(g)')
Question 5 - ChaPter 4, Article 3
you have also asked about Chapter 4, Article 3 (Sections 84300-84307, excluding
Section 84308). These sections prohibit cash contributions of $100 or more' anon)rmous
contributions, money laundering, and the commingling of contributions with personal funds'
(Sections g4300, g+10t, g4304 and g4307.) rn aaaition, contributors and intermediaries must
discrose specified fersonal information whln making a con*ibution, and an agent of a candidate
e Before a CapERS candidate can use pre-effective date contributions in a future election' he or she must
fust deposit them in their campaign bank accouni. For campaign reporting purposes, such payments will be
considered a contribution from the candidate to his or her committee'


File No' A-00-033
Page 8
must promptly report any contribution made to the candidate' (Sections 84302 and 84306')
Since a..contribution" includes contributions made to influence a calPERS election' these
provisions apply to candidates for the calPERS board who receive these contributions'
Section84303requirescandidatesandcommitteestoreportanvexg.endituremadebyan
agent or independent con-tractor on beharf of the candidate o, .o**ittee. Since an "expenditure"
includes payments made to innuence a carpERs election, candidates for the calPERS board
must rePort these PaYments'
Finally, Section 84305 requires candidates to disclose specifiedinformation on carrpaign
mailers. This sender identification requirement appries to "candidates " as defined in Section
8200l,and therefore applies to CaIPERS candidates'
Question 6 - Section 85304
This question relates to the Act',s inter-candidate transfer ban' Section 85304 prohibits
..candidates for erective offrce,, from transfening funds to other candidates in special elections,
and in elections subject to valid.orrt i-Uotioo li'it'''o (Larocque Advice Letter' No' I-98-262')
As candidates for eiective office, .rrr",,t members of CaIPERS who plan to run in the next
election are subject to Section g5304. ise; section g2007.) The inter-candidate transfer ban in
Section g5304 does not appry in carpERS elections unless the carpERs board has established
valid contribution limits' 
I I
Question 7 - Section 84308
Section g430g prohibits board members from soliciting or receiving contributions from
parties and particif*o in specified pro.."aiogs before ttre uoard. (Section 8430so)') In
addition, it requires board members to recuse lhemselves from participating in these proceedings
if they have received confiibutior* oi-L'e than $250 from a party oiparticipant within the 12
months before the decision. (Section gajogo.l Lastly, it requires board members to disclose
such contributions' (/d')
YouwouldliketoknowwhethercontributionstoCa1PERSmembersforelectiontothe
CaIPERS board are ..contributions,, within the coverage of Section 84308. Section 84308 is a
uniqueprovisionbecauseitcontainsdefinitionsthatdonotapplytoanyotherstatutesintheAct.
ro Proposition 208 repealed Section 85304' However' Section 85304 is operative while Proposition 208 is
enjoined.
il ln service Eiployees International (Jnion v. Fair Political Practices commission (9th cil' 1992) 955
F.Zd l3l|,cert. den. 505 U.S. 1230, the court struck down contribution limis based on a fiscal year cycle'


File No. A-00-033
Page 9
For example, section g430g has its own definition of "contribution." under Section 84308, a
..contribution" is a "contribution to candidates and committees in federal, state, or local
elections." (Section 84308(aX6)')
unlike other provisions of the Act, Section 84308 does not apply to contributions
received by "candidates for elective office." Rather, the statute only applies to contributions
received by candidat es rn "federal, state, or local elections." (Section 8a308(aX6)') Section
gzo,zdefines the term ..eliction' ds ,,any primary, general, special or recall_election held in this
stwte.,, A calPERS election is not *.l".tion as defined in Section 82022' consequently'
contributions made or received to inlluence a calPERS election are not subject to Section 84308'
Question 8 - Section 85305
The contribution limit in Section 85305 only applies to "candidates who seek elective
office druing a special election, or a special runoff election'" (Section 85305(a)') while the
elected members of CalpERS hold "elective office," a CaIPERS election is not an "election"
under the Act, as discussed above. (Section gzoz2) Therefore, a calpERS election held outside
calpERS' normar election cyCle is not a..special election'or "special runoff election" for
purposes of Section 85305. As such, candiiates for CaIPERS are not subject to this statute'
Accordingly, contributions made and received to influence a calPERS election are not subject to
the contributions limits in Section 85305'
Other Provisions
Question 9 - Section 87406(c)
The Act places certain restrictions on the activities of certain state officials who are
leaving state service. You hur" ,p..ifically requested advice regarding the one-year ban in
section g7a06(c). Section g7a06(c) piot ititr former."elected state officers" from being paid to
influence specified proceedings before any state administrative agency for one year. The term
..elected state officer,, means any person *t o t old, "elective state office" or has been elected to
an "elective state office." (Section 82O2l.) Under SB 1753' the six elected members of the
l3_member carpERS board hold an ..erective state office" by virhre of their position on the
caIpERS board. (Section g2024.) Therefore, these six members are subject to the prohibition in
Section 87a06(c).
Question 10 - Section 89001
Section gg001 provides that..[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expense." The purpose of this law is io .r,*t that incumbents do not receive an unfair
advantage over chailengers in the next election. (Section 81002(e)')


File No. A-00-033
Page 10
This prohibition applies to mass mailings that feature or make reference to an "elected
officer', affiliated with the "g.n.y 
that produces or sends the mailing. @egulation 18901(aX2)')
The term ..elected officer" means any person who holds an "elective office" or has been elected
to an..elective office" but has not yet taken office. (Section 82020.) Under SB 1753, the six
elected members of the l3-member CaIPERS board hold an "elective office" by virnre of their
position on the calpERS board. (Section 82023.) Therefore, any mass mailing that features or
makes reference to these six members is subject to Section 89001'
Question 1l
Since the elected board members of CaIPERS are subject to the mass mailing prohibition,
you would like to know whether, under the roster listing exception, CaIPERS may distribute a
group photo that includes every CaIPERS board member '
Under Section 89001, a mass mailing may not be sent at public expense if all of the
following four criteria are met, and an exception in Regulation 18901(b) does not apply:
1. The item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her residence, place
of employment or business, or post office box; and the item is a tangible item, such as a
videotape, record, or button, or a written document'
2. Theitem sent either: (1) includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to
an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is
prepared or Sent in cooperatiorr, .orrrultation, coordination' or concert with the elected
officer, or (2) features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing.
3. Any of the costs of distribution are paid for with public moneys; or the costs of design,
production, and printing exceeding $Sti are paid for with public moneys, and the design'
production, or printing is done with ttre intent of sending the item other than as permitted'
4. More than 200 substantially similar items are sent, in a single calendar month'
excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request. @egulation 18901(a)')
you have asked about the roster listing exception in subdivision (b) of Regulation 18901'
Under the roster listing exception, a mass mailing that meets all of the requirements above is not
prohibited if: (1) the elected officer's name only appears in a roster listing containing the names
of all of the elected officers of the agency, (2) the elected officer's name appears in the same type
size, typeface, type color, and location as the names of all of the elected officers of the agency'
and (3) the mailing pates no other impermissible reference to the elected officer' including a
photlgraph of the elected officer' (Regulation 18901(b)(1)')


File No. A-00-033
. 
Page 11
The roster listing exception only applies to the name of the elected officer. Thus, you
may not include a photographof the elected officer in a mass mailing that meets the requirements
in iubdivision (a), unless another exception to the mass mailing prohibition applies.
euestion 12 - Filing Deadlines for Statements of Economic Interests
Under the Act, public officials must disclose their economic interests each year on a
statement of economic interests ("SEI" or "Fortrl700"). Section 87200 lists the public officials
who must report all of their economic interests. (Chapter 7, Article 2.) These officials include,
among otheis, ,.elected state officers," and "officials who manage public investments." All other
officials must report only those economic interests described in their agency's conflict of interest
code. (Chapter i, Artictl :.1 rnus, those who file SEIs under the Act fall into two categories:
Article 2 filers and Article 3 filers.
As ..other public officials who manage public investments," all of the CaIPERS board
members were Article 2 filers before the passage of SB 1753' (Koppes Advice Letter' No'
i-90-517.) ln addition to "other pubiic oifi.iulr who manage public investments," Section 87200
lists..elected state officers." Under SB 1753, the six elected members of the CaIPERS board are
now elected state officers.
Section g1113requires the Commission to specify the filing deadline for SEIs filed
pursuant to Article 2. Regulati or 18723 specifies these deadlines. Subdivision (b)(1) of the
regulation lists the specific state officers who must fiie by March 1. Subdivision (b)(2) provides
that rocal officers must fire by April 1. Finarly, subdivision (b)(3) provides that "other public
officials who manage pubric investnents" must file by April 1. The six elected members of the
CaIPERS board do not fit within any of these three subdivisions' They are not listed in
subdivision (bx1), and subdivision (b)(3) does not apply because they are no longer "other
public officials who manage public investnents." Nevertheless, until the Commission decides to
amend Regulation lg7n,;;advise that the elected members of the CaIPERS board must file
their SEIsiy April-l, ;ilch is the same deadline for all other members of the CaIPERS board'
If you have other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322'5660'
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
Julia Bilaver
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question:
1. Do the campaign requirements in Chapter 4, Article 1 (Sections 84100-84105) apply
to candidates for the CaIPERS board?
2. If so, do the contributions received before the effective date of SB 1753 trigger a duty
to comply with Sections g4100 and g4101? Do the contributions received before the adoption of
Regulations 18451 *J rg+sz trigqer a duty to comply with Sections 84100 and 84101?
3. When must a current elected board membef file a statement of intention to be a
candidate (Form 501)?
4. When must a current elected board member open a campaign bank account?
r Govenrment Code sections g1000 - 91015. ) apply to candidates for the calPERS board?
6. Does the inter-candidate transfer ban in Section 85304 apply to candidates for the
CaIPERS board?
7. Arethe contributions received in a CaIPERS election subject to the contribution
restrictions in Section 84308?
g. Does the special election contibution limit in Section85305 appiy to candidates in a
calpERS election held outside the nonnal election cycle?
9. Are the elected board members of CaIPERS subject to the revolving door laws in
Section 87406(c)?
10. Are the elected board members of CaIPERS subject to the prohibition on mass
mailing at public expense in Section 89001?
ll.Ifso,mayCa1PERSdistributeagroupphotothatincludeseveryCa1PERSboard
member under the roster listing exception of Regulation 18901(b)(1) or (bx8)?

arurual statement of economic interest (Form 700)?
sections.conclusion:
1. Yes. As "candidates" defined in Section 82007, CaIPERS candidates must comply
with the campaign requirements in chapter 4, Article 1 (sections 84100-84105)'
2. Contributions made or received before January 1, 1999, to influence a CaIPERS
election do not count toward qualifying a calPERS candidate as a committee' However' if a

sections.facts:
calpERS administers the public retirement system for califomia's state employees'
classified school employees, and imployees of public agencies that elect to contract with
CalpERS. fn this capacity, baPERS Uofas in trust and is responsible for the investnent of
approximately $1a0iinion. The calpERs, Board of Administration invests the bulk of these
frrnds in the puuri. -*t.ir; it also invests some of these funds in private equity and real estate'
Bystatute,theCa1PERSboardconsistsofthirteenmembers'Ofthese'sixareelected
from the memb"rship ,nder the board's supervision. (S_ection 20100(g).) of the remaining
seven board members, two, the State Treasr:rer and the State controrler, hord ofEce by virtue of
holding statewide elected office. (Section 20100(c) and (d)') The rest of the board members
hold office by appointnent' (Section 20100(a)' (b)' (e)' and (f)')
The six elected calPERS board members are elected by the following constituencies
(1) two are elected by members of the system (i.e., elected by all active and retired calPERS
members, or .'members at large"); (Zj ont is elected by-active state members; (3) one is elected
by active school members; (ai one is elected by active local (except school) members; and
1i; one is elected by retired members' (Section 20100(g)')


Page4
Board members are elected for four-year terms. These terms are staggered. In the first
year of the cycle, calPERS holds an election for the three seats representing active state' school'
and locar members. In the second year, carpERS hords an election for the retiree representative'
There is no election in the third year, and, in the fourth year, calPERS holds an election for the
two member-at-large positions. If a seat becomes vacant during a term' CaIPERS conducts a
special election. gefore sB 1753, CaIPERS developed all of its own election procedures and
under the Act, cand.idates are subject to several rules' For example' they must file a
statement of intention to be a candidate, and maintain a separate campaign bank acco*nt for each
office to which they seek election. (Sections 85200, 85201.) In addition, they must file various
campaign-related reports concerning the monetary activities of their committees' (See e'g''
Sections 84100-8410 5,84225.) Finaily, they must comply with laws that restrict the handling
and receipt of contributions, the making of expenditures, and the distribution of carrrpaign
literatr.re. (Sections 84300-84309')
Before lggg,candidates in CaIPERS elections were not subject to the Act,s provisions
regulating election ,*tpAgD, . (See KoppesAdvice Letter' No' A-g4-l2l')3 However' in 1998'
the Legisrature passed sg izs: tn"t, u*ong other thinqs, amended the Act to require candidates
for the CaIPERS board to file srmFai$ staltements with the Secretary of State' (Stats' 1998' ch'
923.) Therefore, like other candidates for elective office, candidates for the CaIPERS board are
now required to report the monetary activities of their campaign committees' (Section 84225')
calpERS candidates, however, are not subject to the same'disclosure rules as other candidates'
Sections4225,whichimposesthenewreportingrequirementson.CalPERScandidates,
exempts these candidates from chapter +, Article z of the Act (Sections 84200-84',.16',-)- -^,-
(Section 84225@)t Along with gther parts of the Acq Chapter 4' Article 2 sets forth campatgn
discrosrire requirements appricable to arl candidates. The disclosure obligations that apply only
to calpERS candidates are set forth in Regulations 1g451 and 1g452. Regurations 18451 and
lggs1impose limited reporting requiremJnts relative to other candidates'a
policies.
A}{ALYSIS
Campaign Reporting Provisions and Related Restrictions

in law has rendered its advice inapplicable'
,
. Under these regulations, CalpERS candidates must file two semi-annual statements disclosing only the
information specified in the regulatiou'
In addition to adding Section 84225 to the Act, SB 1753 amended the Act's definitions of
..elective offrce,, and "elective state office" to include elected CaIPERS members. (Sections
1ZOZ3,gZO24.) your questions concern the consequences of including elected CaIPERS
members in the Act's aennitions of "elective office" and "elective state office." The legislative
. history of SB 1753 does not reveal the legislative intent behind amending these definitions's In
addition, at a public meeting in October t999, tt " 
Commission declined to narrowly interpret
SB i753 by rejecting propo-sed Regulation 18450.6 The proposed regulation would have
=-pro.ria"d that the eliied members of CaIPERS hold an "electivedEce" and an "elective state
offi.." solely forpurposes of Section 84225'

2
Therefore, under a plain reading of the two statutes, the six elected members of the
CalpERS board are subjecito all of the Act's statutes using the terms "elective office" or
"elective state office," Jxcept Chapter 4, Article 2 as described in Section8a225.ft)'
The first consequence of the new law concerns the Act's definition of "candidate'"
Section gzoo1 defines a..candidate" as "an individual who is listed on the ballot for election to
any elective office." (Emphasis added.) Thus, we conclude that anyone listed as a candidate on a
caIpERS ballot is a.,candidate,, for purposes of the Act, except as limited by Section 84225@)
andRegulations 18451 and 18452'
Section gzool also defines "candidate" to include anyone who receives a contribution or
makes an expenditure '\rith a view to bringing about his or her nomination or election to any
erective ofice.,, @mphasis added.) undeittris second definition, an individual who intends to
become a candidate will be considered one if he or she receives a contribution or makes an
expendinre. This is true even if the individual has not announced his or her candidacy' (Id')
Accordingly, anyone who intends to run for the calPERs board will be considered a "candidate"
when he or she receives a contribution or makes a campaign expendihrre'7
ln general, a "contribution" is a payment made to a"candidate" for the purpose of
influencing the election of the candidate, including a candidate's use of personal funds' (Section
82015; Regulation 18215.) In addition, " puy*.J male by a candidate to influence the election
of the candidate is an "expenditure." (section 8})25;Regulation 18225') Since we have

7 Regulation lg45'l defrnes .,candidate,, as an individual who qualifies for or accepts nomination pursuant

concluded that candidates for the CaIPERS board are "candidates" as defined in Section 82007,
the payments they receive and spend for the election are "contributions" and "expendifures" for

Question 1 - ChaPter 4, Article 1
you have asked whether candidates for the CaIPERS board are subject to the campaign
requirements in Chapter 4, Article 1 (Sections 84100-84105) of the Act'
Section 84100 requires every "committee" to have a teasur"r, who must veriff and sign
the committee's campaign statements. In addition, Sections 84101-84103 require recipient
committees to file a statement of organization (Form 410) with the Secretary of State within 10
days after it has qualified as a committee. A candidate who receives contibutions of $1'000 or
more in a calendar year qualifies as a recipient committee. (Section 82013(a).) As "candidates"
defined by Section'gzool,candidates for the CaIPERS board are subject to Sections 84100-
g4103 if ihey receive contributions of $1,000 or more in a calendar year'
Section g4104 requires candidates to maintain records that are necessary to comply with
the Act. In addition, Section 84105 requires candidates and cornmittees who have received
$5,000 or more from a contributor to notiry the contributor that he or she may be required to file
as a major donor commiffee. (See Section82013(c).) Sections 84104 and 84105 apply to all
.,candidates.,, Since candidates for the CaIPERS board are "candidates" for purposes of the Act
they are subject to these provisions'
Question 2 - Sections 84100 and 84101
Section g4100 requires a CaIPERS candidate to have a treasurer once the candidate
qualifles as a committee. Under Section 84100, a candidate may serve as a treasurer' In
addition, Section g4101 requires the candidate to file a statement of organization within 10 days
after the candidate qualifies as a committee. A candidate qualifies as a cornmittee if he or she
;;;;r """*u*i"* 
of $1,000 or'more in a calendar year' (Section 82013(a)')
you would like to know whether a contribution made to inlluence a CaIPERS election
niggers a calPERS candidate's duty to comply with Sections 84100 and 84101 if it was received
before January 1, 1999, the effective date ofsb n53.E Since the Act did not regulate these
payments before that date, they do not trigger a duty to comply with Sections 84100 and 84101'
s you have also asked about contributions made after the new law took effect, but before the Commission
considercd adopting a narrowing regulation. Any activity in relation to these payments is past couduct' The
Commission does not provide advice ."grraiog-pu,i"o"i""t Cn"gulation 18'29') Moreover' the Commission is
not authorized to waive a statute. (Caminetti u.'S,o'" Uut' tifi ti' Co' Q9a\ 52 Cal'App'2 d32l'325') Therefore'
whether a CalpERS candidate should t ur" .o11rftiJ with th; ect durini that time is not a matter we can address'

However, any contributions received by a CaIPERS candidate after January l, 1999, do
count toward qualifying the candidate as a committee. Therefore, current board members who
intend to rrm in the nex-t election must file a statement of organization within 10 days after
receiving qualifying contributions totaling $1,000 or more.
Question 3 - Section 85200
Section g5200 requires an individual, who intends to be a "candidate for elective office,"
to file a Statement of Intention to be a candidate (Form 501) with the Secretary of State. As
prospective "candidates for elective office," current mernbers of CaIPERS who intend to run in
the next election are subject to this requirement. (See Section 82007') They must file the form
after they decide to nrn ibr reelection, but before they solicit or receive any conffibutions, or
make erpenditures from personal funds on behalf of their candidacy'
Question 4 - Section 85201
Section g5201 requires an individual, who intends to be a "candidate for eiective office"'
to establish a campaign bank account. As prospective "candidates for elective office," cu:rent
members of CaIpERS who intend to run inthe next election are subject to this requirement' (See
Section g2007.) To comply, the candidate must estabrish a campaign bank accorurt after filing a
statement of intention, buttefore making an expenditure. In addition, all monetary contributions
or roans made to the candidate must be Jeposit.a in the account.e (Section 85201(c).) Any
personal funds that the candidate intends to ,r. for his or her election must also be deposited in
tt, campaigu bank account prior to expendittue. (Section 85201(d)')
Section g5201, however, does not require aprospective candidate to open a campaign-
bank account if he or she only receives nonmonetary contributions. In addition, Section 85201
does not apply to candidates who will not receive contributions from others, and will make
expenditui". of p"rronal funds of less than $1,000. (Section 85201(g)')
Question 5 - ChaPter 4, Article 3
you have also asked about Chapter 4, Article 3 (Sections 84300-84307, excluding
Section 84308). These sections prohibit cash contributions of $100 or more' anon)rmous
contributions, money laundering, and the commingling of contributions with personal funds'
(Sections g4300, g+10t, g4304 and g4307.) rn aaaition, contributors and intermediaries must
discrose specified fersonal information whln making a con*ibution, and an agent of a candidate
e Before a CapERS candidate can use pre-effective date contributions in a future election' he or she must
fust deposit them in their campaign bank accouni. For campaign reporting purposes, such payments will be
considered a contribution from the candidate to his or her committee'


File No' A-00-033
must promptly report any contribution made to the candidate' (Sections 84302 and 84306')
Since a..contribution" includes contributions made to influence a calPERS election' these
provisions apply to candidates for the calPERS board who receive these contributions'
Section84303requirescandidatesandcommitteestoreportanvexg.endituremadebyan
agent or independent con-tractor on beharf of the candidate o, .o**ittee. Since an "expenditure"
includes payments made to innuence a carpERs election, candidates for the calPERS board
must rePort these PaYments'
Finally, Section 84305 requires candidates to disclose specifiedinformation on carrpaign
mailers. This sender identification requirement appries to "candidates " as defined in Section
8200l,and therefore applies to CaIPERS candidates'
Question 6 - Section 85304
This question relates to the Act',s inter-candidate transfer ban' Section 85304 prohibits
..candidates for erective offrce,, from transfening funds to other candidates in special elections,
and in elections subject to valid.orrt i-Uotioo li'it'''o (Larocque Advice Letter' No' I-98-262')
As candidates for eiective office, .rrr",,t members of CaIPERS who plan to run in the next
election are subject to Section g5304. ise; section g2007.) The inter-candidate transfer ban in
Section g5304 does not appry in carpERS elections unless the carpERs board has established
valid contribution limits' 
I I
Question 7 - Section 84308
Section g430g prohibits board members from soliciting or receiving contributions from
parties and particif*o in specified pro.."aiogs before ttre uoard. (Section 8430so)') In
addition, it requires board members to recuse lhemselves from participating in these proceedings
if they have received confiibutior* oi-L'e than $250 from a party oiparticipant within the 12
months before the decision. (Section gajogo.l Lastly, it requires board members to disclose
such contributions' (/d')
YouwouldliketoknowwhethercontributionstoCa1PERSmembersforelectiontothe
CaIPERS board are ..contributions,, within the coverage of Section 84308. Section 84308 is a
uniqueprovisionbecauseitcontainsdefinitionsthatdonotapplytoanyotherstatutesintheAct.
ro Proposition 208 repealed Section 85304' However' Section 85304 is operative while Proposition 208 is
enjoined.
il ln service Eiployees International (Jnion v. Fair Political Practices commission (9th cil' 1992) 955
F.Zd l3l|,cert. den. 505 U.S. 1230, the court struck down contribution limis based on a fiscal year cycle'
For example, section g430g has its own definition of "contribution." under Section 84308, a
..contribution" is a "contribution to candidates and committees in federal, state, or local
elections." (Section 84308(aX6)')
unlike other provisions of the Act, Section 84308 does not apply to contributions
received by "candidates for elective office." Rather, the statute only applies to contributions
received by candidat es rn "federal, state, or local elections." (Section 8a308(aX6)') Section
gzo,zdefines the term ..eliction' ds ,,any primary, general, special or recall_election held in this
stwte.,, A calPERS election is not *.l".tion as defined in Section 82022' consequently'
contributions made or received to inlluence a calPERS election are not subject to Section 84308'
Question 8 - Section 85305
The contribution limit in Section 85305 only applies to "candidates who seek elective
office druing a special election, or a special runoff election'" (Section 85305(a)') while the
elected members of CalpERS hold "elective office," a CaIPERS election is not an "election"
under the Act, as discussed above. (Section gzoz2) Therefore, a calpERS election held outside
calpERS' normar election cyCle is not a..special election'or "special runoff election" for
purposes of Section 85305. As such, candiiates for CaIPERS are not subject to this statute'
Accordingly, contributions made and received to influence a calPERS election are not subject to
the contributions limits in Section 85305'
Other Provisions
Question 9 - Section 87406(c)
The Act places certain restrictions on the activities of certain state officials who are
leaving state service. You hur" ,p..ifically requested advice regarding the one-year ban in
section g7a06(c). Section g7a06(c) piot ititr former."elected state officers" from being paid to
influence specified proceedings before any state administrative agency for one year. The term
..elected state officer,, means any person *t o t old, "elective state office" or has been elected to
an "elective state office." (Section 82O2l.) Under SB 1753' the six elected members of the
l3_member carpERS board hold an ..erective state office" by virhre of their position on the
caIpERS board. (Section g2024.) Therefore, these six members are subject to the prohibition in
Section 87a06(c).
Question 10 - Section 89001
Section gg001 provides that..[n]o newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expense." The purpose of this law is io .r,*t that incumbents do not receive an unfair
advantage over chailengers in the next election. (Section 81002(e)')
This prohibition applies to mass mailings that feature or make reference to an "elected
officer', affiliated with the "g.n.y 
that produces or sends the mailing. @egulation 18901(aX2)')
The term ..elected officer" means any person who holds an "elective office" or has been elected
to an..elective office" but has not yet taken office. (Section 82020.) Under SB 1753, the six
elected members of the l3-member CaIPERS board hold an "elective office" by virnre of their
position on the calpERS board. (Section 82023.) Therefore, any mass mailing that features or
makes reference to these six members is subject to Section 89001'
Question 1l
Since the elected board members of CaIPERS are subject to the mass mailing prohibition,
you would like to know whether, under the roster listing exception, CaIPERS may distribute a
group photo that includes every CaIPERS board member '
Under Section 89001, a mass mailing may not be sent at public expense if all of the
following four criteria are met, and an exception in Regulation 18901(b) does not apply:
1. The item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at his or her residence, place
of employment or business, or post office box; and the item is a tangible item, such as a
videotape, record, or button, or a written document'
2. Theitem sent either: (1) includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to
an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or sends the mailing, and is
prepared or Sent in cooperatiorr, .orrrultation, coordination' or concert with the elected
officer, or (2) features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing.
3. Any of the costs of distribution are paid for with public moneys; or the costs of design,
production, and printing exceeding $Sti are paid for with public moneys, and the design'
production, or printing is done with ttre intent of sending the item other than as permitted'
4. More than 200 substantially similar items are sent, in a single calendar month'
excluding any item sent in response to an unsolicited request. @egulation 18901(a)')
you have asked about the roster listing exception in subdivision (b) of Regulation 18901'
Under the roster listing exception, a mass mailing that meets all of the requirements above is not
prohibited if: (1) the elected officer's name only appears in a roster listing containing the names
of all of the elected officers of the agency, (2) the elected officer's name appears in the same type
size, typeface, type color, and location as the names of all of the elected officers of the agency'
and (3) the mailing pates no other impermissible reference to the elected officer' including a
photlgraph of the elected officer' (Regulation 18901(b)(1)')


. 
The roster listing exception only applies to the name of the elected officer. Thus, you
may not include a photographof the elected officer in a mass mailing that meets the requirements
in iubdivision (a), unless another exception to the mass mailing prohibition applies.
euestion 12 - Filing Deadlines for Statements of Economic Interests
Under the Act, public officials must disclose their economic interests each year on a
statement of economic interests ("SEI" or "Fortrl700"). Section 87200 lists the public officials
who must report all of their economic interests. (Chapter 7, Article 2.) These officials include,
among otheis, ,.elected state officers," and "officials who manage public investments." All other
officials must report only those economic interests described in their agency's conflict of interest
code. (Chapter i, Artictl :.1 rnus, those who file SEIs under the Act fall into two categories:
Article 2 filers and Article 3 filers.
As ..other public officials who manage public investments," all of the CaIPERS board
members were Article 2 filers before the passage of SB 1753' (Koppes Advice Letter' No'
i-90-517.) ln addition to "other pubiic oifi.iulr who manage public investments," Section 87200
lists..elected state officers." Under SB 1753, the six elected members of the CaIPERS board are
now elected state officers.
Section g1113requires the Commission to specify the filing deadline for SEIs filed
pursuant to Article 2. Regulati or 18723 specifies these deadlines. Subdivision (b)(1) of the
regulation lists the specific state officers who must fiie by March 1. Subdivision (b)(2) provides
that rocal officers must fire by April 1. Finarly, subdivision (b)(3) provides that "other public
officials who manage pubric investnents" must file by April 1. The six elected members of the
CaIPERS board do not fit within any of these three subdivisions' They are not listed in
subdivision (bx1), and subdivision (b)(3) does not apply because they are no longer "other
public officials who manage public investnents." Nevertheless, until the Commission decides to
amend Regulation lg7n,;;advise that the elected members of the CaIPERS board must file
their SEIsiy April-l, ;ilch is the same deadline for all other members of the CaIPERS board'

g. No. The special election contribution limits do not apply to CaIPERS elections held
